geographic_scope: All 50 United States, including Alaska and Hawaii insets; tracked facilities use city-level display points
time_horizon: quarterly from 2026-Q3 through 2030-Q4; annually from 2031 through 2040

source_versions: OpenStates v3 discovery interface — credential required

data_run.status: partial — explicit gaps and blockers retained

county.source: U.S. Census Geocoding Services Current_Current, accessed 2026-07-21

huc8.source: USGS Watershed Boundary Dataset Map Service, accessed 2026-07-21

principal_aquifer_context.source: USGS 2003 Principal Aquifers data release, WFS accessed 2026-07-21
principal_aquifer_context.analytical_status: regional spatial context only; not evidence of facility water supply or groundwater use

balancing_authority_screen.source: HIFLD public-use Control Areas snapshot dated 2021-12-08, queried 2026-07-21
balancing_authority_screen.analytical_status: provisional, low confidence, and excluded from forecast inputs pending serving-utility/topology confirmation

retail_utility_screen.source: HIFLD public-use Electric Retail Service Territories snapshot dated 2025-08-21, queried 2026-07-21
retail_utility_screen.analytical_status: provisional, low confidence, and excluded from forecast inputs pending provider, tariff, contract, and interconnection confirmation

source_dataset.name: Epoch AI — AI Data Centers
source_dataset.included_scope: full 65-record U.S. source snapshot preserved; 18-record curated subset is rendered in the public interface

limitations: Facility MW and H100 equivalents are estimates, not meter readings
Completion weights are demonstration priors pending hindcast calibration
Principal-aquifer and control-area polygons are spatial screening context, not confirmed service or supply relationships
Reported EIA, USGS, and drought conditions are context and do not automatically change model parameters
No policy or news item changes a forecast without primary text, effective dates, quantitative support, and reviewed GitHub approval
